entity.summary: Centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched around 2018. Altilly suffered a December 2020 hosting-account compromise in which attackers gained administrative access, deleted servers and backups, stole high-value hot-wallet assets, and left much of the remaining exchange data and wallet state inaccessible; HEI retains inactive status because no clean terminal shutdown announcement is established.
entity.notes: The December 2020 breach is now event-backed. Available preserved statements describe stolen assets, deleted production servers and backups, inaccessible funds, and a later claims process, but do not provide a definitive permanent-shutdown date. Current Altilly-branded domains are not treated as proof of original exchange continuity.

expected.summary: Centralized cryptocurrency exchange associated with the Altilly brand. Public exchange trackers describe Altilly as launched around 2018 and currently untracked / inactive. The exchange was affected by a late-2020 security breach, after which public references indicate the service became inaccessible or effectively non-operational.
expected.notes: HEI models Altilly as inactive rather than dead because public trackers and exchange-review references support a security-breach disruption and inactive / untracked status, but the current evidence set does not establish a clean official shutdown announcement or terminal date. Some current Altilly-branded domains may be cloned or repurposed and should not be treated as proof of original exchange continuity.

changes.summary: Centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched around 2018. Altilly suffered a December 2020 hosting-account compromise in which attackers gained administrative access, deleted servers and backups, stole high-value hot-wallet assets, and left much of the remaining exchange data and wallet state inaccessible; HEI retains inactive status because no clean terminal shutdown announcement is established.
changes.notes: The December 2020 breach is now event-backed. Available preserved statements describe stolen assets, deleted production servers and backups, inaccessible funds, and a later claims process, but do not provide a definitive permanent-shutdown date. Current Altilly-branded domains are not treated as proof of original exchange continuity.

events.title: Altilly detected the compromise that destroyed exchange infrastructure
events.description: Altilly detected suspicious activity on December 23, 2020 before attackers gained administrative access, stole high-value hot-wallet assets, deleted production servers and backups, and left remaining exchange wallet data inaccessible.
events.notes: Preserved statements distinguish the initial December 23 detection from the later December 25-26 escalation. No single reliable loss estimate is used because public figures conflict.

evidence.title: Altilly Hot Wallets Hacked and Cold Wallets Lost
evidence.notes: Case file preserves extensive exchange statements describing suspicious activity from December 23, server compromise, deleted infrastructure and backups, stolen hot-wallet assets, and the claims process.
